name: Best of the Border Barbecue Brisket-Austin Am
recipeIngredient: 5 tbsp Best of the Border Dry Rub
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat barbecue pit to 250 degrees.
2. Cut off a fat wedge near the large end of the brisket, then pat it dry evenly over the meat.
3. Place the brisket in the barbecue pit, fat side up, and cook at 250 to 275 degrees for 4 hours.
4. Remove the meat from the pit.
5. Make a foil bag large enough to hold the brisket with extra-heavy-duty foil, leaving one side open.
6. Put the meat in the bag and add beer. Seal all edges of the foil well.
7. Return the brisket in the bag to the pit. Raise the temperature to 300 degrees.
8. Let the pit cook back down to 225 to 250 degrees and cook another 3 hours.
9. Remove the brisket from the pit, top with favorite barbecue sauce.
10. Slice and serve. Serves 16 to 20.
-- Texas Barbecue
From: Bluebeard #103 @4903 6 Date: 08-10-01
